quality: Removes what blocks progress — but also PLACES obstacles for those who are not ready
story_role: The first puja — invoked before any new beginning
character_archetype: The threshold guardian — determines who is ready to pass
notes: Heroes who face impossible blockages that turn out to be tests
column10: The first trial of every hero's journey is Vighneshvara at work

quality: Leading the ganas — the imperfect beings who together form something greater
story_role: Commander of groups — the one who organises the diverse
character_archetype: The one who makes the group function — the connective power
notes: Heroes who unite unlikely allies — who make teams from broken people
column10: Essential for Karnataka and Jharkhand heroes (elephant + tribal unity)

quality: Leading without needing to be seen leading — the quiet hand that guides
symbol: His own broken tusk used as a pen
story_role: Writer of the Mahabharata — the scribe who had no ego about the work
character_archetype: The ghost leader — the one who makes others great
notes: Heroes who enable others to succeed rather than seeking personal glory
column10: Guru characters in MahaHulmane often embody Vinayaka

quality: Breaking one's own tusk to continue the work — sacrificing part of oneself to complete the mission
symbol: Single intact tusk + the broken tusk he holds
story_role: The Mahabharata scribe who broke his own tusk when his pen broke
character_archetype: The one who gives up something real — not symbolically — to finish the task
notes: Heroes who sacrifice something irreplaceable to complete their duty
column10: For stories where winning costs the hero something permanent

quality: Stomach that holds all experience — every grief joy memory and future possibility
story_role: The one who has digested all experience and is not broken by it
character_archetype: The elder who has seen everything and is still gentle
notes: Mentor/guru characters — those who carry great knowledge lightly
column10: The most underestimated form — wisdom hidden in the ordinary

quality: The curved path that reaches what the straight path cannot — wisdom that winds around obstacles
symbol: Curved trunk probing into hidden spaces
story_role: Often painted with trunk curved to the left (unusual — drawing attention)
character_archetype: The one who solves problems sideways — the unexpected approach
notes: Heroes who win through indirection — Rajasthan (Mrigatrishna) most connected
column10: For puzzle-solving stories — the trick that works when force cannot

quality: The one who grants siddhi (accomplishment) to those who have done the work
story_role: Radiant — gold — seated in full lotus
character_archetype: The reward that comes after the work is truly done — not before
notes: The mentor who appears at the completion — not the beginning
column10: This is the form invoked at tattoo level 4 — the completion form

quality: Love of clear thinking — the one who rewards those who reason truly
story_role: Often depicted with a book in one hand
character_archetype: The patron of careful thought over impulsive action
notes: Tripura hero (Tripurari) and scholars/engineers in the world
column10: For characters who win through thinking — not fighting

quality: The mighty who choose the smallest vehicle — because they can ride anything and choose humility
story_role: God of the universe riding a mouse — the cosmic joke of power and humility
character_archetype: The moment of deliberate smallness — choosing the humble path when you could choose the grand one
notes: Heroes who win by making themselves small when everyone expects them to be large
column10: For Sikkim hero (Kanchenjunga) who chooses non-violence

quality: The five-headed form who protects those who have no other protector — fierce love for the powerless
symbol: Five heads + multiple weapons + lion as vehicle
story_role: Five-headed — lion-riding — fierce expression unlike the gentle usual Ganesha
character_archetype: The protector who becomes terrifying in service of those who cannot protect themselves
notes: Heroes who fight specifically for those the system has abandoned
column10: For Chhattisgarh (Dandakarni) and Jharkhand (Vanajara) — protectors of marginalised
